channel.title: Productivity on Max Scheijen
channel.description: Recent content in Productivity on Max Scheijen

item.title: Run Custom Scripts From Anywhere
item.description: Lately I started to look into creating simple scripts that can automate some tasks for me. So I started looking into shell scripting. I wanted to run and have access to these scripts from anywhere in my terminal. After watching a lot of Mischa van den Burg I started taking in interest in more thorough note taking. My first script would be for easier note taking. So I took his blog script tweaked it a little, and wanted to make it executable from anywhere in my terminal.

item.title: Finding files using the terminal
item.description: Finding notes or files on your system can be hard. Where did you put that file? We can use command line tools to search for files. One of the easiest ways to find files on your system based on their name is using fd. fd [PATTERN] This will return all the relevant files with your current directory. It wil also ignore the patterns from .gitignore by default. Another nice tool to find files is fzf which is fuzzy finder.
